article.title: Apple's AirPods Max 2 Bring the H2 Chip and Live Translation Five Years After the Original, With the Same 385-Gram Shell
article.summary: Apple's first AirPods Max refresh since 2020 swaps in the H2 chip and inherits AirPods Pro 3 features while keeping the unchanged industrial design, weight, and 20-hour battery life.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Apple refreshed its premium over-ear headphones last month for the first time since December 2020, quietly unveiling the AirPods Max 2 on March 16 with a new H2 chip, improved active noise cancellation, and the suite of intelligent audio features it had previously reserved for its in-ear AirPods Pro 3. The company [began shipping the $549 headphones in early April](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-introduces-airpods-max-2-powered-by-h2/) in five colors — midnight, starlight, orange, purple, and blue — with pre-orders opening March 25.

The launch arrived without the usual cycle of leaks or rumors. [TechCrunch described the reveal as a "quiet" unveiling](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/16/apple-quietly-launches-airpods-max-2/) that caught the market off guard, with the product rolling out across more than 30 countries and regions.

## What's New

The AirPods Max 2 swap the original model's H1 chip for the H2, the same silicon that powers Apple's latest earbuds. Apple says the new chip makes active noise cancellation ["up to 1.5x more effective"](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-introduces-airpods-max-2-powered-by-h2/) than the first-generation AirPods Max, paired with new computational audio algorithms and a revised transparency mode.

The H2 also unlocks several features that the original over-ear model never received:

- **Adaptive Audio**, which automatically blends ANC and Transparency based on the user's environment
- **Conversation Awareness**, which lowers volume and reduces background noise when the wearer begins speaking
- **Voice Isolation** for clearer calls
- **Live Translation**, powered by Apple Intelligence, for in-person conversations across languages
- **Studio-quality audio recording**, **Camera Remote**, **Loud Sound Reduction**, **Personalized Volume**, and Siri head-gesture controls

Apple [confirmed support for 24-bit, 48 kHz lossless audio](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-introduces-airpods-max-2-powered-by-h2/) when the headphones are connected via the included USB-C cable, paired with a new high dynamic range amplifier. The lossless specification matches the cabled capability of the 2020 model, which gained USB-C support in a 2024 hardware revision.

## What Hasn't Changed

The exterior is untouched. In its [review, 9to5Mac measured the AirPods Max 2 at 386.2 grams](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/31/airpods-max-2-review/) — essentially identical to the original — with the same aluminum ear cups, stainless steel headband, and mesh canopy. The headphones still do not fold, which the publication flagged as a recurring storage problem, and the rated battery life remains 20 hours of listening time.

The price is also unchanged at $549, the figure that drew criticism when the first AirPods Max launched with the aging H1 chip. [9to5Mac called the AirPods Max 2 a "meaningful update"](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/31/airpods-max-2-review/) that finally modernizes the product line but noted it "won't convert skeptics of the original design." The review described the audio-quality gains as a "slight improvement" with cleaner sound and better spatial separation, while acknowledging that the ANC upgrade is the most immediately noticeable change in everyday use.

## What We Don't Know

Apple has not published detailed figures for the new high dynamic range amplifier or quantified the claimed latency reductions for wireless gaming. The Live Translation feature's regional and language availability [varies by market](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-introduces-airpods-max-2-powered-by-h2/), and Apple has not disclosed a full rollout schedule for supported languages. Sales figures for the first AirPods Max, which Apple rarely breaks out separately from the broader AirPods line, remain undisclosed, making it difficult to gauge how much pent-up demand the five-year gap created.

## Market Context

The refresh keeps Apple's flagship over-ear headphones at the top of the premium price bracket without matching the weight or battery life of rivals from Sony and Bose, tradeoffs the company has so far declined to address with a redesigned chassis. Instead, the generational jump focuses almost entirely on silicon and software: the same physical product, running the same intelligence stack as the AirPods Pro 3, five years on from the original's debut.
